/sports/sports_league/teams./sports/spor.description: Connects a sports league or confederation to the national teams that participate in it.

/sports/sports_league_draft/picks./sport.description: Connects a sports league draft to the school from which a drafted player was selected.

/sports/sports_position/players./america.description: Connects a American football position category to a specific playing position used in historical football rosters.

/sports/sports_position/players./sports/.description: Connects a sports position category to a playing position of athletes in a team roster.
Connects a sports position to the team that has players in that position on its roster.

/sports/sports_team/colors.description: Indicates the official colors associated with a sports team.

/sports/sports_team/roster./american_foo.description: Connects an American college football team to a player position that has been part of its historical roster, specifically describing positions such as running back within American football. This relation is used in the context of U.S. football programs like NCAA teams.
/sports/sports_team/roster./american_foo.head_relation_tail: <american football team> includes players in position <football position>
/sports/sports_team/roster./american_foo.reverse_relation: <football position> is played in <american football team>

/sports/sports_team/roster./american_foo.description: Connects a American football team to the playing position of athletes on its roster.

/sports/sports_team/roster./baseball/bas.description: Connects a baseball team to the playing position of athletes on its roster.

/sports/sports_team/roster./basketball/b.description: Connects a basketball team (NBA) to the playing position of athletes listed on its roster, such as power forward, within the league structure.

/sports/sports_team/sport.description: Connects a sports team (such as a football club) to the type of sport it participates in, such as association football (soccer).

/sports/sports_team_location/teams.description: Connects a sports team to the city or location where the team is based or represents, including professional leagues like the NBA.

/time/event/instance_of_recurring_event.description: Connects a specific event instance to the recurring event series it belongs to, such as an annual award ceremony.
/time/event/instance_of_recurring_event.head_relation_tail: <event instance> is an instance of <recurring event>

/time/event/locations.description: Connects a historical or recurring event to the geographical locations where it took place or had significant activity.

/travel/travel_destination/climate./trav.description: Connects a travel destination to a specific month in its monthly climate profile, describing seasonal weather conditions for that period.

/travel/travel_destination/how_to_get_he.description: Connects a travel destination to available transportation methods used to reach it, such as train, flight, or bus.

/tv/non_character_role/tv_regular_person.description: Connects a TV non-character role (such as contestant, guest, or participant) to the person who appears in that role on a television program.

/tv/tv_network/programs./tv/tv_network_d.description: Connects a television network (such as ABC) to TV programs that have been broadcast or aired by that network.

/tv/tv_personality/tv_regular_appearance.description: Connects a TV personality to a television program in which they make a regular appearance or participate, such as a competition or reality show.

/tv/tv_producer/programs_produced./tv/tv.description: Connects a TV producer to the type of production role they held on television programs, such as executive producer, indicating their level of responsibility in TV production.
/tv/tv_producer/programs_produced./tv/tv.reverse_relation: <producer type> is a role held by <producer>

/tv/tv_producer/programs_produced./tv/tv.description: Connects a television production company (such as a studio or producer) to the TV programs it has produced or contributed to, including animated and live-action series.

/tv/tv_program/country_of_origin.description: Connects a television program (including sitcoms, dramas, and other TV series) to the country where it was originally produced or originated.

/tv/tv_program/genre.description: Connects a television program (such as an animated series, sitcom, or drama) to its genre classification, describing the style or thematic category of the show.

/tv/tv_program/languages.description: Connects a television program to the primary language(s) used in its dialogue and production, typically reflecting the language of broadcast.

/tv/tv_program/program_creator.description: Connects a television program (including animated or live-action series) to the person who created or developed it.

/tv/tv_program/regular_cast./tv/regular_.description: Connects a television program to an actor who is part of its regular cast, including voice actors in animated series or live-action performers.

/tv/tv_program/tv_producer./tv/tv_produc.description: Connects a television program (such as a sitcom or series) to the type of producer role involved in its production, such as executive producer, showrunner, or co-producer.

/tv/tv_writer/tv_programs./tv/tv_program.description: Connects a television writer to a TV program they have contributed to, including animated series and live-action shows.

/user/alexander/philosophy/philosopher/i.description: Connects a philosopher to a field of philosophical interest or area of study they focus on, such as epistemology, ethics, or metaphysics.

/user/jg/default_domain/olympic_games/sp.description: Connects an edition of the Olympic Games to a sport or discipline that was included in its competition program, such as show jumping in equestrian events.

/user/ktrueman/default_domain/internatio.description: Connects an international organization (such as the European Union) to its member states that belong to the organization.

/user/tsegaran/random/taxonomy_subject/e.description: Connects a subject or entity to a classification system used to categorize knowledge domains, such as the Library of Congress Classification.
